preamble: The shift from a glottal stop [ʔ] to a voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] is a rare but documented example of phonetic strengthening or assimilation, particularly in Afro-Asiatic languages.

examples.note: Assimilatory shift triggered by nearby pharyngeal or ejective sounds.
Documented interchange in specific rural lexical items.

sources: Linguistic studies on Ethio-Semitic consonant harmony
Sociolinguistic surveys of Algerian Arabic dialects
